comments.body:
> Something must have gone wrong, a `+15,343 −4,498` backport seems rather risky. Looking at all the bumps in dependencies (storage even to a non-release), I think some other backports are required before - assuming this backport is needed.

I believe that a backport is needed, as I mentioned in https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/13536#issuecomment-1073865883.

I can cherry pick the actual fix into the https://github.com/containers/storage/tree/release-1.37 branch and then vendor that one?

> I can cherry pick the actual fix into the https://github.com/containers/storage/tree/release-1.37 branch and then vendor that one?

Yes, that would be great, thank you!
LGTM once the storage change merges

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *dcermak*, *mheon* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/v3.4/OWNERS)~~ [mheon] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Could someone restart the CI? The tests failed with an unrelated infra issue:
```
# podman pull quay.io/libpod/testimage:20210610
Trying to pull quay.io/libpod/testimage:20210610...
Error: initializing source docker://quay.io/libpod/testimage:20210610: Requesting bear token: invalid status code from registry 500 (Internal Server Error)
```

@rhatdan The `Optional Release Test` CI test fails with:
```
ERROR: Releases must never contain '-dev' in output of 'podman info' (  APIVersion: 3.4.5-dev
  Version: 3.4.5-dev)  (contrib/cirrus/runner.sh:268 in _run_release())
```
Should I look into fixing this?
Can you run podman -v and see if this shows the -dev flag, if not, then I would not worry about it.

> Can you run podman -v and see if this shows the -dev flag, if not, then I would not worry about it.

It shows it:
```ShellSession
❯ ./bin/podman -v
podman version 3.4.5-dev
```

The "Optional Release Test" is an optional test as the name states, it must be triggered manually. However running it on random PRs is wrong, it is only designed to be run on release PRs to make sure the version is correctly set.

The result can be ignored.

/hold cancel
